text: Hi, this is Dr. Michael Franks showing you a video of a robotic right radical nephrectomy. This is a very pleasant female with an eight centimeter central renal tumor.

Following entry into the abdomen, the surgeon mobilizes the colon away from the kidney. The vena cava is identified, running left to right below the scissors. Dissection occurs between the vena cava and Gerota's fascia.

The surgeon uses the robotic third arm to elevate tissue, continuing dissection downward to identify the ureter and gonadal vein. The gonadal vein is visible, and further dissection reveals the ureter, which is then dissected and clipped.

The surgeon identifies the psoas muscle posterior to the kidney, marking the limit of dissection in a relatively avascular plane. The robotic fourth arm is utilized to elevate the kidney and ureter during the dissection process.

The surgeon tracks upwards towards the renal hilum, focusing on the renal artery and vein. The renal vein is clearly visible and being dissected, running from the right lower to the left upper part of the surgical field.
The surgeon dissects the small renal artery, clipping it with WAC clips. The artery is further separated from surrounding lymphatic tissue, with visible venous branches located behind it.
The surgeon clips the identified structure, applying two clips on each side for secure closure, as part of the routine procedure.

The surgeon applies one final clip for vascular control of the kidney, ensuring no significant veins or arteries are visible behind the well-controlled artery. The laparoscopic stapler is prepared to proceed with venous control.
The surgeon places the kidney under slight tension to create a clear line for stable application of the Ethicon stapler with a vascular load, preparing to control the renal vein.

The surgeon navigates through the adrenal tissue, sparing it as they proceed with the dissection. The adrenal tissue is located north or to the right of the stapler, with no visible adrenal involvement in the procedure.

The surgeon carries the dissection laterally towards the side wall, scoring the lateral attachments of the kidney to facilitate its removal. The kidney is placed in a retrieval bag and extracted through a small incision near the umbilicus, completing the radical nephrectomy procedure.
